regime_fall.current_status: Following collapsed Islamabad peace talks on April 12, 2026, the Trump administration has implemented a comprehensive naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz with massive military deployments (7,000-8,000 troops plus warplanes), while Iran appointed hardliner Zolghadr as security chief and signals military readiness despite severe economic strain and internal political fracturing between President Pezeshkian and the IRGC.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump announced a sweeping naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz on April 12, 2026, with orders to interdict ships and clear naval mines, coupled with deployment of 1,000 additional troops, 2,000 82nd Airborne paratroopers, and two Marine combat groups totaling 5,000-6,000 troops to the Middle East.
